Many authors have borrowed the phrase ""World enough and time"" from the poem's opening line to use in their book titles. The most famous is Robert Penn Warren's 1950 novel ""World Enough and Time: A Romantic Novel,"" about murder in early-19th-century Kentucky. With variations, it has also been used for books on the philosophy of physics (""World Enough and Space-Time: Absolute versus Relational Theories of Space and Time""), geopolitics (""World Enough and Time: Successful Strategies for Resource Management""), a science-fiction collection (""Worlds Enough & Time"").
